Rajat's Blog

Concepts to remember - DL

Batch Normalization

Batch Normalization (BatchNorm) is a technique introduced to improve the training of deep neural networks. It normalizes the output of a previous activation layer by subtracting the batch mean and dividing by the batch standard deviation. This helps in reducing internal covariate shift, where the distribution of network activations changes during training, and can speed up training and improve convergence.

  • Normalization: For each feature map batchnorm computes the mean and SD across the batch. It then normalizes the feature maps

  • Scaling and Shifting: After normalization, the output is scaled and shifted by leanable params. This is achieved through parameters `gamma` and `beta`, which are learned during training
